ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "position" TEXT /* Job title or position of the employee */,
  "office" TEXT /* Office location of the employee */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the employee */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started working */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the employee */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's direct manager */,
  "status" TEXT /* Employment status (e.g., active, terminated) */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was terminated, if applicable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_office FOREIGN KEY ("office") REFERENCES offices ("office_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_position FOREIGN KEY ("position") REFERENCES positions ("position_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "positions" (
  "position_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each position */,
  "position_name" TEXT /* Name of the position */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the position */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the position belongs to */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the position */,
  "required_education" TEXT /* Required education level for the position */,
  "required_experience" INTEGER /* Required years of experience for the position */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("position_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_positions_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "offices" (
  "office_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each office */,
  "office_name" TEXT /* Name of the office */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the office */,
  "address" TEXT /* Detailed address of the office */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the office */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the office */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("office_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department's manager */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Budget allocated to the department */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the office location where the department is based */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES offices ("office_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee affected by the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date the change occurred */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., promotion, salary change) */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value after the change */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value before the change */,
  "change_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_history_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_history_change_by FOREIGN KEY ("change_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR, manager, executive) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Encrypted password of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the user in a comma-separated list */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the employee data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Specific action performed (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
